commandsRun: cd "/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile" && flutter test test/features/threads/thread_detail_page_test.dart --plain-name "open-on-Mac success and failure are surfaced clearly"

cd "/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile" && flutter test test/features/threads/thread_detail_page_test.dart --plain-name "desktop integration toggle updates open-on-Mac affordances"

cd "/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile" && flutter test test/features/settings/settings_page_test.dart --plain-name "desktop integration toggle persists across relaunch"

cd "/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile" && flutter test test/features/settings/runtime_notification_delivery_controller_test.dart --plain-name "cold-start notification delivery stays fail-closed until preferences restore"

assertions.reason: The Flutter thread-detail harness passed the open-on-Mac success/failure flow, and the live read-only bridge POST returned 200 with the same thread_id plus a matching codex://thread URL, confirming the mobile action targets the matching underlying thread.

The widget harness kept the thread detail flow usable while surfacing best-effort open results, and the live bridge response explicitly reported best_effort=true with the message that desktop refresh/open is best effort and mobile remains usable.
The targeted Flutter widget test passed the failure branch where the second open-on-Mac attempt surfaces a clear user-facing error banner without collapsing the thread detail surface.

Desktop integration toggle persistence passed across a simulated relaunch in the settings widget harness, and the cold-start notification controller test restored persisted disabled notification preferences before permitting delivery, confirming settings remain in effect after reopen.

The targeted thread-detail widget test showed the open-on-Mac button enabled initially, disabled with a visible desktop-integration-disabled message when the toggle was turned off, and re-enabled immediately when the toggle was turned back on.
